hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 6, Q. Your opponent raised before the cards were dealt. You called.
River — 2. Your opponent fired a large river bet.

wrongReasons: 5 2 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that 5 2 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
4 7 makes a full house here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
J 3 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that J 3 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
hand.explanation:
Answer: K K

Your opponent flopped two pair and bet all three streets with growing sizing. The consistent pressure — especially the larger turn bet — signals a hand strong enough to protect but not quite a monster.
